name: AVASCULAR NECROSIS
definition: AVN = OSTEONECROSIS = ASEPTIC NECROSIS consequence of reduced / completely interrupted blood supply to bone with death of cellular focal sclerosis + osteopenia normal normal / barely detectable trabecular mottling; abnormal bone scan / MRI subchondral undermining ("crescent" sign) + cyst formation mild alteration in femoral head contour / subchondral fracture + normal joint marked collapse of femoral head + significant acetabular involvement joint space narrowing + acetabular degenerative changes TIBIA VARA avascular necrosis of medial tibial condyle VERTEBRAL OSTEOCHONDROSIS = VERTEBRA PLANA avascular necrosis of vertebral body osteochondrosis of head of 2nd (3rd / 4th) metatarsal LUNATOMALACIA avascular necrosis of lunate bone normal radiographs + abnormal MRI increased radiographic density with preservation of normal lunate shape + extensive carpal degenerative changes avascular necrosis of tarsal scaphoid COXA PLANA idiopathic avascular necrosis of femoral head in children; one of the most common sites of histologic + clinical diagnosis without radiographic findings sclerosis ± cystic changes with preservation of contour + surface of femoral head benign self-limited disorder of fragmented ossification in epiphysis of humeral capitellum nontraumatic spontaneous osteonecrosis of entire scaphoid OSTEONECROSIS OF PROXIMAL POLE OF SCAPHOID SONK
